requirements: The core contributions of the paper 'The Illusion of Equivalence: Systematic FP16 Divergence in KV-Cached Autoregressive Inference' have been reproduced.

sub_tasks.requirements: The environment and infrastructure setup has been correctly configured.
Code has been implemented using PyTorch 2.5.1 with CUDA 12.1 and HuggingFace Transformers 4.53.0.

verification.spec: Run `python -c 'import torch, transformers; print(torch.__version__, torch.version.cuda, transformers.__version__)'` and assert torch version starts with '2.5.1', CUDA version starts with '12.1', and transformers version starts with '4.53.'.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to set CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG=:4096:8 and enable deterministic behavior via torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True) for greedy decoding to ensure bitwise reproducibility.

verification.spec: Grep the repo for 'CUBLAS_WORKSPACE_CONFIG' set to ':4096:8' and for a call to `torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True)`. Both must be present and executed in the greedy decoding setup code path.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code is configured to run on a single NVIDIA H100 NVL (95 GB) GPU (or comparable hardware) to keep hardware-dependent nondeterminism controlled.

verification.spec: Run `nvidia-smi -L` and verify an H100 GPU is present. Additionally check that the model loading code does not require multi-GPU sharding (single-device placement).

sub_tasks.requirements: Models and datasets have been acquired and prepared.
Code has been implemented to load LLaMA-2-7B in FP16 using HuggingFace AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained.

verification.spec: Run the model loading code and assert the loaded model is an instance of LlamaForCausalLM, that model.config.hidden_size==4096 and num_hidden_layers==32, and that model.dtype == torch.float16. Verify load was done via AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to load Mistral-7B-v0.3 in FP16 using HuggingFace AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained.

verification.spec: Run the model loading code and assert the loaded model is a Mistral model (MistralForCausalLM), num_key_value_heads=8 with num_attention_heads=32 (GQA ratio 4:1), and model.dtype == torch.float16. Verify load via AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to load Gemma-2-2B in FP16 using HuggingFace AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained.

verification.spec: Run the model loading code and assert the loaded model is a Gemma2ForCausalLM with head_dim==256 and sliding window attention enabled in config, and model.dtype == torch.float16. Verify load via AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to load the GSM8K mathematical reasoning benchmark test set (1,319 examples).

verification.spec: Import the data loading module and verify it loads the GSM8K test set, returning 1,319 examples each with a question and answer field.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to randomly sample 700 examples without replacement from GSM8K using SAMPLE_SEED=42.

verification.spec: Import the sampling utility, call it with SAMPLE_SEED=42, and verify it returns 700 unique GSM8K examples (no duplicates). Also verify reproducibility by calling twice and checking identical outputs.

sub_tasks.requirements: The custom token-by-token generation loop and decoding strategies have been correctly implemented.
Code has been implemented for greedy decoding (T=0.0) which selects the argmax token at each step.

verification.spec: Inspect the decoding-strategy config for greedy: assert it performs argmax sampling (no temperature scaling or equivalently temperature=0) and produces deterministic output for identical inputs.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for top-k sampling with k=50 and T=0.7.

verification.spec: Inspect top-k sampling config and assert k==50 and temperature==0.7 are set at the sampling step.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for nucleus (top-p) sampling with p=0.95 and T=0.7.

verification.spec: Inspect top-p sampling config and assert p==0.95 and temperature==0.7 are set at the sampling step.

sub_tasks.requirements: Divergence metrics have been correctly implemented.
Code has been implemented for token divergence: a binary indicator of whether cache-ON and cache-OFF produce different token sequences at any decode step.

verification.spec: Import the token_divergence function and verify with synthetic test cases: identical token sequences return 0/False; sequences differing at any position return 1/True.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute per-step KL divergence between the cache-ON softmax distribution p_t and the cache-OFF softmax distribution q_t over the full vocabulary at each decode step.

verification.spec: Call the KL computation routine on two known softmax distributions (e.g., p=softmax(logits_on), q=softmax(logits_off)) and verify it returns sum(p * (log p - log q)) over the full vocab, computed per decode step. Cross-check against torch.nn.functional.kl_div on a synthetic example.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute per-step Jensen-Shannon divergence between p_t and q_t with m_t = 0.5(p_t + q_t).

verification.spec: Import the JS divergence function and verify on test cases: JS(p,p)=0; JS(p,q) in [0,1]; symmetric JS(p,q)=JS(q,p); m=(p+q)/2 is used as the mixture. Compare against scipy.spatial.distance.jensenshannon squared.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute the flip index: the first decode step at which cache-ON and cache-OFF differ on a token.

verification.spec: Call the flip-index function on two synthetic token sequences (e.g., on=[1,2,3,5,6], off=[1,2,3,4,6]) and assert it returns 3 (the first differing position, 0-indexed) or 4 (1-indexed) consistent with paper's convention. Also verify it handles identical sequences (returns sentinel) and immediate divergence (returns 0/1).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute mean KL across all decode steps as the primary magnitude measure.

verification.spec: Import the mean KL function and verify with a synthetic KL trajectory (e.g., array of 128 values) that it returns the arithmetic mean across decode steps.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute GSM8K binary correctness (accuracy) for generated outputs.

verification.spec: Import the GSM8K correctness scoring function and run on synthetic prediction/answer pairs: correct numerical answer should yield 1, incorrect should yield 0. Verify handling of GSM8K's '#### answer' format.

sub_tasks.requirements: Statistical analysis tools have been correctly implemented.
Code has been implemented for paired McNemar's test on cache-ON vs cache-OFF correctness with Bonferroni and BH-FDR correction at α=0.05.

verification.spec: Import the McNemar test wrapper and verify on synthetic paired correctness data: (a) symmetric disagreement returns p~1; (b) systematic disagreement returns small p. Verify Bonferroni and BH-FDR multiple comparison correction is applied at alpha=0.05.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for bootstrap 95% confidence intervals for mean KL and accuracy delta.

verification.spec: Import the bootstrap CI function and verify with a synthetic sample (e.g., normal data) that it returns 95% confidence intervals close to the analytical interval for mean KL and accuracy deltas.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for Mann-Whitney U tests with BH-FDR correction on per-layer hidden state drift magnitudes.

verification.spec: Import the layer drift significance test and verify it runs Mann-Whitney U per layer on synthetic drift data, returns one p-value per layer, and applies BH-FDR correction across 90 layer-model combinations.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for Pearson and Spearman correlations between flip index and mean KL divergence, with partial correlations excluding flip index = 1 and flip index ≤ 3.

verification.spec: Import the correlation analysis function and verify on synthetic (flip_index, mean_KL) data it returns Pearson r, Spearman rho, and partial correlations after filtering out flip_index==1 and flip_index<=3 subsets.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented for Welch's t-test comparing mean KL of early-flip (bottom 33rd percentile) vs late-flip (top 33rd percentile) groups.

verification.spec: Import the early-vs-late flip Welch's t-test function. Given synthetic flip_index and mean_KL arrays, verify it splits at 33rd and 67th percentiles, runs scipy.stats.ttest_ind with equal_var=False, and returns the t-statistic and p-value.

sub_tasks.requirements: Experiment 1: Behavioral Characterization has been executed and analyzed.
Code has been implemented to classify each example as always-diverges, sometimes-diverges, or never-diverges across the five seeds.

verification.spec: Import the classification function. Given synthetic 5-seed divergence indicators for a set of examples, verify it correctly labels each as 'always-diverges' (5/5), 'sometimes-diverges' (1-4/5), or 'never-diverges' (0/5).

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced mean KL on LLaMA-2 greedy is approximately 10.68 (within reasonable tolerance).

verification.spec: Run LLaMA-2-7B greedy decoding on 3500 GSM8K run pairs (700 examples x 5 seeds), compute mean KL across all decode steps and run pairs. Report mean KL.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced mean KL on LLaMA-2 top-k is approximately 10.70.

verification.spec: Run LLaMA-2-7B in FP16 over 3,500 run pairs (700 examples x 5 seeds) under top-k sampling (k=50, T=0.7, max_new_tokens=128). For each run pair, compute per-step KL divergence between cache-ON and cache-OFF softmax distributions; take the mean across all 128 steps. Verify the mean across run pairs is approximately 10.70.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced mean KL on LLaMA-2 top-p is approximately 10.79.

verification.spec: Run LLaMA-2-7B in FP16 over 3,500 run pairs under top-p sampling (p=0.95, T=0.7, max_new_tokens=128). Compute per-step KL between cache-ON and cache-OFF distributions, take the mean across steps per pair, and verify the mean across run pairs is approximately 10.79.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced mean KL on Mistral greedy is approximately 11.46.

verification.spec: Run Mistral-7B-v0.3 greedy decoding on 3500 GSM8K run pairs (700 examples x 5 seeds), compute mean KL across all decode steps and run pairs. Report mean KL.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced mean KL on Mistral top-k is approximately 10.70.

verification.spec: Run Mistral-7B-v0.3 in FP16 over 3,500 run pairs under top-k sampling (k=50, T=0.7, max_new_tokens=128). Compute mean KL across steps per pair, verify the mean across run pairs is approximately 10.70.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced mean KL on Mistral top-p is approximately 10.90.

verification.spec: Run Mistral-7B-v0.3 in FP16 over 3,500 run pairs under top-p sampling (p=0.95, T=0.7, max_new_tokens=128). Compute mean KL across steps per pair, verify mean across run pairs is approximately 10.90.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced mean KL on Gemma greedy is approximately 12.66.

verification.spec: Run Gemma-2-2B greedy decoding on 3500 GSM8K run pairs (700 examples x 5 seeds), compute mean KL across all decode steps and run pairs. Report mean KL.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced mean KL on Gemma top-k is approximately 12.80.

verification.spec: Run Gemma-2-2B in FP16 over 3,500 run pairs under top-k sampling (k=50, T=0.7, max_new_tokens=128). Compute mean KL across steps per pair, verify mean across run pairs is approximately 12.80.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced mean KL on Gemma top-p is approximately 12.83.

verification.spec: Run Gemma-2-2B in FP16 over 3,500 run pairs under top-p sampling (p=0.95, T=0.7, max_new_tokens=128). Compute mean KL across steps per pair, verify mean across run pairs is approximately 12.83.

sub_tasks.requirements: On Gemma greedy, the cache-ON vs cache-OFF accuracy difference is non-significant (around p=0.43) and is the only condition where cache-OFF accuracy (~2.1%) exceeds cache-ON (~1.9%).

verification.spec: Run Gemma-2-2B greedy decoding cache-ON vs cache-OFF on the GSM8K subset (3500 pairs). Verify that the McNemar test yields p>=0.05 (around 0.43) and that cache-OFF accuracy (~2.1%) is greater than cache-ON (~1.9%) — i.e. it is the only condition with reversed direction and non-significant difference.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to plot the mean KL divergence trajectory across all 128 decode steps for each model and strategy (reproducing Figure 1), confirming divergence persists without converging to zero.

verification.spec: Verify that a plotting script exists which loads per-step KL trajectories (128 decode steps) for all 3 models x 3 strategies and produces a figure with subplots showing mean KL vs decode step. Import the script and confirm it produces a matplotlib Figure with the expected number of subplots/lines.

sub_tasks.requirements: JS divergence trends mirror KL divergence trends across all conditions, confirming KL findings are not artifacts of KL's unbounded range.

verification.spec: For each of the 9 model-strategy conditions, compute both mean KL divergence and mean JS divergence over all run pairs. Verify that the rank-order of conditions by JS divergence matches (or strongly correlates with, Spearman rho > 0.8) the rank-order by KL divergence.

sub_tasks.requirements: Experiment 2: Layer Drift Analysis has been executed and analyzed.
Code has been implemented to compute per-layer hidden state drift as the L2 norm of the difference vector between cache-ON and cache-OFF hidden states at decode step 1.

verification.spec: Import the per-layer drift computation function. Provide synthetic cache-ON and cache-OFF hidden states (known difference vector) at decode step 1, and verify the function returns the L2 norm of (h_on - h_off) per layer matching the analytical L2 norm within 1e-5.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute per-layer cosine similarity between cache-ON and cache-OFF hidden states.

verification.spec: Import the per-layer cosine similarity function. Provide synthetic cache-ON and cache-OFF hidden states with known cosine similarities (e.g., orthogonal, identical, 45-degree) per layer, and verify the function returns values matching analytical cosine within 1e-5.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to compute per-head attention weight KL divergence to isolate where divergence appears.

verification.spec: Import the per-head attention weight KL function. Provide two synthetic [H, T] attention weight distributions (softmaxed) with a known KL per head and verify the function returns the analytically expected KL values per head within 1e-5.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced LLaMA-2 hidden state drift increases from approximately 3.45 at layer 0 to approximately 361.96 at layer 1 (≈105× amplification).

verification.spec: On LLaMA-2-7B in FP16, on a subset of high-divergence GSM8K examples, capture hidden states at every transformer layer for both cache-ON and cache-OFF at decode step 1. Compute L2 norm of (h_on - h_off) per layer averaged over examples. Report drift at layer 1.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced Mistral hidden state drift increases from approximately 25.76 at layer 0 to approximately 902.36 at layer 1 (≈35× amplification).

verification.spec: On Mistral-7B-v0.3 in FP16, on a subset of high-divergence GSM8K examples, capture hidden states at every transformer layer for both cache-ON and cache-OFF at decode step 1. Compute L2 norm of (h_on - h_off) per layer averaged over examples. Report drift at layer 1.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced Gemma per-layer hidden state drift is approximately flat across all 26 layers (between ≈1.0 and ≈1.4), with no sudden amplification event.

verification.spec: On Gemma-2-2B in FP16, on a subset of high-divergence GSM8K examples, capture per-layer L2 hidden state drift at decode step 1 across all 26 layers. Assert that the per-layer drift values fall within the flat range approximately [1.0, 1.4] with no single-layer amplification jump > 5x. Report the mean per-layer drift across layers.

sub_tasks.requirements: Mann-Whitney U tests (BH-FDR corrected) show 89 of 90 layer-model combinations are significant, with only Gemma layer 16 not significant (p≈0.877).

verification.spec: Run Mann-Whitney U tests on per-layer drift magnitudes for each (model, layer) combination comparing across high-divergence examples vs baseline. Apply BH-FDR correction across all 90 layer-model combinations at alpha=0.05. Count significant combinations. Expected: 89/90 significant, with Gemma layer 16 the only non-significant (p~0.877).

sub_tasks.requirements: Gemma cosine similarity starts at approximately 0.85 and drops near 0 by layer 13 (cosine ≈ 0.009), with non-monotonic recovery in later layers.

verification.spec: Run Gemma-2-2B in FP16 with cache-ON vs cache-OFF on a high-divergence GSM8K prompt. Capture per-layer hidden states at decode step 1 for both modes. Compute cosine similarities per layer. Verify layer 0 cosine ~0.85 and that the minimum cosine across layers 0-13 reaches near 0 (close to 0.009).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to produce the per-layer hidden state drift plot reproducing Figure 2.

verification.spec: Verify that a plotting script exists which loads per-layer drift values for all 3 models and produces Figure 2 (L2 drift vs layer index, one curve per model). Import the script, run it, and confirm a matplotlib Figure is generated with 3 curves.

sub_tasks.requirements: Experiment 4: Decision Boundary Analysis has been executed and analyzed.
Code has been implemented to split run pairs into early-flip (bottom 33rd percentile of flip index) and late-flip (top 33rd percentile) groups and compute mean KL per group.

verification.spec: Import the early/late-flip splitting function. Provide a synthetic table of (flip_index, mean_KL) pairs and verify the function returns the bottom-33rd-percentile group as early-flip and top-33rd-percentile group as late-flip, with the correct group sizes and computed per-group mean KL values matching numpy.percentile-based manual computation.

sub_tasks.requirements: Welch's t-test confirms early-flip examples have higher mean KL than late-flip examples in 8 of 9 conditions (p<0.05), with ratios in the range 1.006-1.043.

verification.spec: For each of the 9 model-strategy conditions, split run pairs into early-flip (bottom 33%) and late-flip (top 33%) by flip index, then run Welch's t-test comparing mean KL between groups. Count conditions where early-flip mean KL > late-flip mean KL with p<0.05 AND the ratio (early/late) lies in [1.006, 1.043]. Expected: 8 of 9.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced full Pearson correlation between flip index and mean KL for LLaMA-2 is approximately r = -0.202 (p < 10⁻⁹⁶).

verification.spec: Run LLaMA-2-7B over 10,500 run pairs (700 examples x 5 seeds x 3 strategies or equivalent), compute flip index and mean KL per pair, then compute the Pearson correlation between flip index and mean KL. Verify r is approximately -0.202 with p < 1e-96.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced full Pearson correlation between flip index and mean KL for Mistral is approximately r = -0.152 (p < 10⁻⁵⁵).

verification.spec: Run Mistral-7B-v0.3 over 10,500 run pairs, compute flip index and mean KL per pair, then compute Pearson correlation. Verify r is approximately -0.152 with p < 1e-55.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced full Pearson correlation between flip index and mean KL for Gemma is approximately r = -0.120 (p < 10⁻³⁴).

verification.spec: Run Gemma-2-2B over 10,500 run pairs, compute flip index and mean KL per pair, then compute Pearson correlation. Verify r is approximately -0.120 with p < 1e-34.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced partial Pearson correlation excluding flip index ≤ 3 strengthens for Mistral (≈ -0.333) and Gemma (≈ -0.213), and weakens for LLaMA-2 (≈ -0.115).

verification.spec: For each model, compute Pearson partial correlation between flip_index and mean_KL after excluding run pairs with flip_index<=3 (n~10,500 per model before exclusion). Expected: LLaMA-2 r~-0.115, Mistral r~-0.333, Gemma r~-0.213. Verify Mistral and Gemma magnitudes increase versus the full correlation, and LLaMA-2 magnitude decreases.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to produce the decision boundary analysis four-panel plot reproducing Figure 4 (scatter, group means, |r| under exclusion, flip-index distributions).

verification.spec: Verify a plotting script exists that produces the 4-panel Figure 4 from the decision boundary analysis data: (a) scatter of flip_index vs mean KL, (b) bar/box of early- vs late-flip mean KL per model, (c) |r| bar plot under three exclusion conditions per model, (d) histogram/violin of flip_index per model. Run and confirm 4 axes are generated.

sub_tasks.requirements: Experiment 5: Activation Patching (Causal Localization) has been executed and analyzed.
Code has been implemented to select the n=600 highest-KL examples per model (in decreasing order of mean KL from the full 700).

verification.spec: Import the selection routine. Provide a synthetic table of 700 (example_id, mean_KL) pairs and verify the function returns the top-600 example_ids sorted by mean KL in decreasing order, matching numpy.argsort()[-600:][::-1] over the synthetic data.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to inject cache-ON hidden states into the cache-OFF forward pass at decode step 0, for a single layer at a time.

verification.spec: Import the activation patching routine. For a small model (or stub), run two forward passes (cache-ON, cache-OFF) at decode step 0 capturing hidden state h_on at a target layer L. Then run cache-OFF again with a forward hook that overwrites layer L's hidden output with h_on. Verify the patched layer L output equals h_on (within fp tolerance) while non-patched layers remain unchanged.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to cumulatively patch layers 0 to L simultaneously in the cache-OFF forward pass.

verification.spec: Import the cumulative patching function and verify it accepts a parameter L specifying the top layer index, patches cache-ON hidden states into cache-OFF forward pass at decode step 0 for all layers 0..L simultaneously, and returns the resulting KL divergence. Test on a small model with L=0,1,2 and confirm that as L increases, more layers are patched (verifiable by hooks counting patch operations).

sub_tasks.requirements: Activation patching experiments are run with MAX_STEPS=32 decode steps per example.

verification.spec: Grep the activation patching experiment code/config for MAX_STEPS=32 (or equivalent constant) and verify it is passed to the patching loop. Confirm by instrumenting the decode loop to count steps and asserting count == 32.

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced median single-layer recovery across all layers and models is approximately -0.019%.

verification.spec: Run activation patching on all three models (LLaMA-2-7B, Mistral-7B-v0.3, Gemma-2-2B) in FP16 on the top n=600 highest-KL GSM8K examples per model. For each example, perform single-layer residual stream patching: inject cache-ON hidden states into the cache-OFF forward pass at decode step 0, one layer at a time, for MAX_STEPS=32 decode steps. Compute recovery as (KL_unpatched - KL_patched) / KL_unpatched * 100%. Pool single-layer recovery values across all layers and all three models, then compute the median. Verify the median is approximately -0.019% (within tolerance 0.5 percentage points).

sub_tasks.requirements: The reproduced mean single-layer recovery across all layers and models is approximately -1.21%.

verification.spec: From the same activation patching results as the previous leaf, compute the mean (not median) percentage recovery across all layers, examples, and all 3 models. Verify mean is approximately -1.21%.

sub_tasks.requirements: Gemma's median single-layer recovery is approximately +5.75%, distinctly higher than LLaMA-2 (≈ +0.08%) and Mistral (≈ -8.83%), with median range across Gemma layers staying steady at 5.73%-5.85%.

verification.spec: Run single-layer activation patching across all layers of Gemma-2-2B on the n=600 highest-KL GSM8K examples in FP16. Compute the median recovery percentage across all 26 layers. Verify the median is approximately +5.75% and falls within the range 5.73%-5.85%.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to verify that prefill produces identical hidden states under cache-ON and cache-OFF (||h_on - h_off|| = 0 at all layers), so divergence first appears at decode step 0.

verification.spec: Run prefill on a sample prompt using LLaMA-2-7B (or any test model) with cache-ON and cache-OFF execution paths in FP16. Extract hidden states at all layers after the prefill phase (before decode step 0). Verify ||h_on - h_off|| == 0 exactly at every layer.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented to produce the activation patching plot reproducing Figure 5 showing per-layer recovery percentages for all three models.

verification.spec: Run the plotting script and verify it produces a figure file containing per-layer recovery percentage curves for LLaMA-2, Mistral, and Gemma. Check the figure has three distinct series (one per model), x-axis is layer index, y-axis is recovery percentage, and labels/legend mention the model names.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that cumulative activation patching of all residual stream layers in LLaMA-2-7B yields a mean recovery of -5.34% (negative recovery confirming patching increases divergence)

verification.spec: Run activation_patching(model='llama-2-7b', layers='all', n_examples=600, max_steps=32) computing recovery = 1 - KL(patched, cache_off)/KL(cache_on, cache_off); assert |measured - (-5.34)| / 5.34 <= 0.30 and measured < 0

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that cumulative activation patching of all residual stream layers in Gemma-2-2B yields a mean recovery of -2.88%

verification.spec: Run activation_patching(model='gemma-2-2b', layers='all', n_examples=600, max_steps=32); assert |measured - (-2.88)| / 2.88 <= 0.30 and measured < 0

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that single-layer activation patching of Mistral-7B-v0.3 residual stream yields a median recovery of -8.83% (negative single-layer median)

verification.spec: For each transformer layer, patch cache-ON hidden state into cache-OFF forward pass at decode step 0; compute per-layer recovery; report median across layers; assert |measured - (-8.83)| / 8.83 <= 0.30 and measured < 0

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Gemma-2-2B per-layer single-layer patching median recovery has a lower bound of approximately 5.73% across the 26 layers

verification.spec: Run single-layer patching across all 26 Gemma-2-2B layers; compute per-layer median recovery; report minimum of these medians; assert |measured - 5.73| / 5.73 <= 0.30

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that Gemma-2-2B per-layer single-layer patching median recovery has an upper bound of approximately 5.85% across the 26 layers

verification.spec: Run single-layer patching across all 26 Gemma-2-2B layers; compute per-layer median recovery; report maximum of these medians; assert |measured - 5.85| / 5.85 <= 0.30

sub_tasks.requirements: Ablation studies from the paper

Code has been implemented and run such that the FP32 falsification ablation is performed: rerun the full cache-ON vs cache-OFF inference pipeline in FP32 on n=600 GSM8K examples per model, demonstrating flip rate drops to exactly 0.0% across all three models

verification.spec: Load each of LLaMA-2-7B, Mistral-7B-v0.3, Gemma-2-2B in torch.float32; run cache-ON vs cache-OFF on 600 GSM8K examples with max_new_tokens=32; assert flip_rate == 0.0 for all three models

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that per-head attention weight KL divergence is isolated and measured between cache-ON and cache-OFF, verifying divergence appears in attention patterns vs accumulated hidden values

verification.spec: Hook each attention layer to capture softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d)) per head under cache-ON and cache-OFF; compute KL divergence per head per layer; report mean KL is positive and significantly above FP32 noise floor (>1e-5)

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that FP16 rounding error in K and V projections is measured by comparing joint computation (cache-OFF) against token-by-token computation (cache-ON), confirming the error originates at cache write time

verification.spec: Compute K and V projections for a prefix two ways: (1) jointly over the whole sequence (cache-OFF style), (2) token-by-token appending one position at a time (cache-ON style); compute L2 norm of difference; assert difference is nonzero (>1e-5) and ~0.036% relative

sub_tasks.paper_unit: % mean relative FP16 K/V projection error

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that multi-layer FP16 error propagation is simulated through N sequential attention and residual steps, showing single-layer rounding errors compound across layers to produce KL divergence on the order observed in the behavioral characterization

verification.spec: Construct a synthetic stack of N attention+residual blocks in FP16; inject a single FP16 rounding error at layer 0; propagate through N layers; measure cumulative divergence norm grows with N and produces final KL on order ~10 (matching observed)

sub_tasks.requirements: Method components (from critique splits and additions)

Code has been implemented and run such that, on the configured GPU, computing (a+b)+c vs a+(b+c) in FP16 over representative attention dot-product magnitudes produces measurable non-associativity, demonstrating the floating-point non-associativity property underlying the paper's central mechanism.

verification.spec: Generate a sequence of FP16 values matching attention score magnitudes; compute sum in two different orderings (left-to-right vs pairwise tree); assert the two results differ by at least 1 ULP, demonstrating non-associativity.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that mean per-step KL divergence between cache-ON and cache-OFF softmax distributions is computed over the full vocabulary at each of 128 decoding steps and recorded as a trajectory per model and strategy.

verification.spec: Implement a token-by-token generation loop that returns full-vocab logits at each step for both cache-ON and cache-OFF; compute KL(p_t || q_t) per step; assert output is a length-128 trajectory of finite non-negative floats.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that statistical analyses (McNemar tests with Bonferroni and BH-FDR correction at alpha=0.05; bootstrap 95% confidence intervals for mean KL and accuracy deltas) are applied to the behavioral results.

verification.spec: Implement McNemar test with Bonferroni correction, BH-FDR correction, and bootstrap 95% CI; verify that for the 9 model-strategy conditions the corrected p-values and bootstrap intervals are computed and stored.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that activation patching recovery percentage is defined and computed as (KL_baseline - KL_patched)/KL_baseline * 100, with negative values indicating that patching increases divergence.

verification.spec: Implement the recovery metric; given KL_baseline and KL_patched arrays, return signed percent recovery; assert the function correctly returns negative values when KL_patched > KL_baseline.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented such that the cache-ON forward pass uses use_cache=True and threads past_key_values across decoding steps (rather than recomputing the prefix each step)

verification.spec: Inspect cache-ON generation loop source; assert model(...) is called with use_cache=True and past_key_values argument is updated each step from outputs.past_key_values; assert input_ids passed each step (after step 0) has length 1, not full prefix length

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that cache-ON inference only computes K/V for the single new token at each decode step, verified by inspecting that the KV cache length grows by exactly 1 per decode step and that the input_ids fed to the model at step t>0 contain only one token.

verification.spec: Instrument the cache-ON decode loop: at each step t>=1, assert input_ids shape is (batch, 1); after the forward pass, assert past_key_values[layer][0].shape[-2] == prefix_len + t (i.e., cache length grew by exactly 1). Run for at least 8 decode steps on a small prompt and assert all checks pass.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the cache-ON KV cache stores K and V tensors in FP16 dtype matching the model's loaded dtype.

verification.spec: Load model with torch_dtype=torch.float16. Run one cache-ON decode step. Assert past_key_values[0][0].dtype == torch.float16 and past_key_values[0][1].dtype == torch.float16 for all layers.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that the cache-OFF decoding path calls the model with use_cache=False at every decode step.

verification.spec: Monkey-patch or wrap the model's forward call in the cache-OFF decode loop to record the use_cache kwarg. Run >=4 decode steps and assert that every recorded call had use_cache=False (and that returned past_key_values is None).

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that cache-OFF inference re-feeds the full token sequence (original prefix + all previously generated tokens) at every decode step.

verification.spec: Instrument the cache-OFF loop: at decode step t (zero-indexed), assert input_ids.shape[-1] == prefix_len + t, and assert the first prefix_len tokens equal the original prompt tokens and the remaining tokens equal the sequence of tokens generated so far. Run for >=8 steps and assert all checks pass.

sub_tasks.requirements: Code has been implemented and run such that cache-OFF recomputes K and V for all positions on every step (no past_key_values reuse), verified by output equivalence to an independent from-scratch forward on the same full token sequence.

verification.spec: At a chosen decode step in the cache-OFF loop, capture the next-token logits L1. Independently run model(input_ids=full_sequence, use_cache=False, past_key_values=None) from scratch and capture next-token logits L2. Assert torch.allclose(L1, L2, atol=0, rtol=0) (bitwise equal), confirming no cached K/V was reused. Repeat for >=3 different decode steps.
